test_output_summary.runner_output: Test Files 95 passed (95) | Tests 554 passed (554) | Duration 135.15s
key_tests: Deve soft-deletar um usuário (ele some das leituras)
Deve soft-deletar mesmo com check-ins (sem bloqueio)

notes.RF-006_physical_preservation: UseCase calls user.delete() [sets _deletedAt = new Date()] then userRepository.update(user) — UPDATE path, no physical DELETE. No userRepository.delete() call anywhere in soft-delete path. Row stays in DB.
notes.RF-006_inmemory_filter: InMemoryUserRepository.update() replaces entity in-set via delete+add. userOfId() filters: !user.isDeleted && user.id === id — returns null only because isDeleted=true, NOT because row was removed. users Set still contains the entity.
notes.RF-006_migration: Migration 20260531140844_add_user_deleted_at adds only: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN deleted_at TIMESTAMP(3). No CASCADE DELETE. Additive-only schema change preserves all existing rows and FK relationships.
notes.RF-010_checkin_unblocked: DeleteUserUseCase has zero dependency on CheckInRepository or UnitOfWork. No FK constraint check on check-ins before soft-delete. Test 'Deve soft-deletar mesmo com check-ins (sem bloqueio)' confirms success with check-in data present.
notes.user_entity: User.isDeleted getter: return this._deletedAt != null. User.delete() method: this._deletedAt = new Date(). Pure in-memory mutation, persisted via update().
